key_insight: PF-DIC integrates phase-field fracture concepts into digital image correlation to measure displacement, strain, and crack damage in one unified full-field framework.
engineering_relevance: This enables more accurate displacement measurement near cracks, automatic crack handling without manual masks, and better support for damage assessment in brittle and fracture-prone structures.
method: Use a phase-field variable to represent crack evolution and complex crack morphologies.
Embed damage into the DIC optimization so displacement, strain, and crack fields are estimated together.
Implement the formulation with finite elements for experimental speckle-image analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical examples showing the method can capture different crack types, improve displacement accuracy near crack tips, and selectively identify critical cracks under different loading conditions.
watch_item: The approach adds model and implementation complexity, so robustness and calibration may be challenging for noisy images or highly complex experimental setups.
paper_title: PF-DIC: Phase field digital image correlation for integrated full-field displacement, strain, and damage measurements
